title: Vuffi Raa
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Droid |type= |image=[[File:VuffiNew.png]] |name=Vuffi Raa |homeworld= |birth= |death= |creator=[[The One (being)|The One]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' |manufacturer=Civilization of the [[Silentium]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |line= |model=Unique creation |class=[[Class Two multi-phasic robot]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' |cost= |length= |width= |height=1 meter |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]] |pronouns= |sensor=Red |plating= |armament= |equipment=*Detachable tentacles *Fingers/subfingers *[[Microfusion pile]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *Multi-[[Spectrum/Legends|spectrum]] [[Photoreceptor/Legends|Photoreceptor]] *Optic sensors |affiliation=*[[Silentium]] *[[The One (being)|The One]] *[[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] *[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] }} {{Quote|Don't call me Master!|Lando Calrissian, to Vuffi Raa|}} '''Vuffi Raa''' was a specially designed and constructed [[Droid/Legends|droid]] thought to be an agent of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] droid [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Silentium]], who created him to explore [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and learn about other civilizations for his "parental" droids. In one of Vuffi Raa's early encounters, he was reprogrammed by the shape-shifting [[Croke]] [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|sorcerer]] [[Rokur Gepta]]. Gepta gave him to an agent, [[Osuno Whett/Legends|Osuno Whett]], who used him in the field. He served Whett until asked to perform an operation that had him labeled as a "butcher" and sought refuge on a desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. It was there that he encountered [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who had won him in a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]], and after serving out a program to betray Lando, began his new life as a vagrant wanderer and partner to Calrissian. Vuffi Raa was programmed by his creators to be completely non-violent, and even piloting a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in battle stressed him. However, he eventually became one of the few droids to overcome his own programming, and became able to attack in defense of himself and his friends. ==Description== Vuffi Raa was a mixture between [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] life and advanced droid technology, designed to resemble [[Silentium's creators|the star-shaped original creators]] of his "species." He had been created by an interstellar being known as [[The One (being)|The One]] for the purpose of exploring the rest of the galaxy and learning about other civilizations. [[File:Vuffi_Raa_Diagram.png|thumb|left|150px|A diagram of Vuffi Raa.]] Vuffi Raa stood one meter tall and resembled an attenuated starfish with five elongated [[Chromite/Legends|chromite]] tentacles. His main body was pentagonal in shape and made of polished chromite. In the center of his body was a powerful multi-frequency [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]] capable of seeing in [[Ultraviolet/Legends|ultraviolet]] and [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] wavelengths. Vuffi also had a range of other [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and a miniature [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] hidden in his underside. Furthermore, he was powered by a [[microfusion pile]], a near-infinite source of power. His five tentacles each tapered into a point at the end, and then divided into five-tentacled hands, with one tiny optic sensor in each palm. At the end of each tentacle, Vuffi Raa's fingers continued to split into microscopic strand fingers which allowed him to manipulate minuscule objects. He could also direct heat into his tentacles, and was able to detach all five from his body and control them remotely. These functions were facilitated by a thick, blood-like liquid that circulated throughout the droid's systems, keeping them lubricated and allowing coagulation in the event the outer shell was ever punctured. He could also repair himself. Vuffi Raa's name was actually a number in the language of the extinct species who designed his droid progenitors, and he detested being referred to as just "Vuffi". ==Biography== [[File:VuffiRaa1-FF126.png|thumb|right|Vuffi Raa with some technical details]] ===Early life=== Vuffi Raa was an intricately-designed and constructed [[Droid/Legends|droid]] that was thought to be an agent of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extragalactic]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Silentium]]. This starship-sized race of droids were able to fly from one side of the galaxy to the next, and built their culture around the primary numbers of one, five, seven and eleven. They had been manufactured by an asteridian species who had gone extinct when the sun of their planet went [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]]; their droids lived on and eventually achieved full sentience. Members of his kind included beings referred to as [[The One (being)|The One]] and [[The Other]]; others were collectively called [[Silentium|The Rest]]. Long ago in their home galaxy, the droids of the Silentium waged a protracted war against the [[Abominor]], another droid species that was based upon asymmetry and chaos. Vuffi Raa was created to explore the galaxy and learn about other civilizations for his parental droids. Early in his career, he was captured and reprogrammed by the [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifting]] [[Croke]] [[Rokur Gepta]], a [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] and the [[Scrivinir]] of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]. Rokur Gepta then gave Vuffi Raa to his agent ''[[Ottdefa/Legends|Ottdefa]]'' [[Osuno Whett/Legends|Osuno Whett]].''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' On one occasion, following the rediscovery of the [[Renatasia system/Legends|Renatasia system]]—a lost [[Human/Legends|Human]] colony founded by ancient settlers from [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]]—shortly after the foundation of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Whett was tasked by the Empire and the Centrality to win the trust of the [[Renatasian]]s so as to make them easy targets for the [[Imperial Military/Legends|armed forces]] to subdue. Covering the droid with an organic exterior, he posed as Vuffi Raa's assistant and presented themselves as representatives of a galactic civilization eager to welcome the Renatasians. Vuffi Raa and Whett toured the world and helped the Renatasians form a unified world government. Then, after 700 days, Whett transmitted all of his data, beginning the invasion by Imperial and Centrality forces. Because of the [[Battle of Renatasia|massacre]] that followed, Vuffi Raa was nicknamed the "Butcher of Renatasia," as none of the Renatasians realized he was a droid and that Whett was behind the event. Fearing for his life, Whett radically changed his appearance. He lost forty [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]], added four [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] of [[bonemer]] to each of his [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and legs, and added a vertebra. He also changed the style of his hair, which turned itself white from worry. Whett hid Vuffi Raa on [[Rafa IV]], in the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]]. ===A new master=== {{Quote|How ''should'' I call you, sir?''"
"''Not too loudly, Vuffi Raa, and no earlier than nine-hundred in the morning.|Vuffi Raa and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]|}} [[File:VuffiRaa-FF.jpg|thumb|left|A bottom shot of Vuffi Raa.]] Later, Whett played [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] with [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] during Lando's visit to [[Oseon 2795]], in which Lando won Vuffi Raa, though he had to travel to Rafa IV to acquire the droid. However, before he could depart, he was arrested on trumped-up charges by [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Duttes Mer]] and forced to aid Rokur Gepta in locating the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] of Sharu. Provided with a transdimensional key, Calrissian chose to begin his search on [[Rafa V]], location of the largest [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] pyramid in the Rafa system. He was ambushed by several Toka, who tied him to one of the crystalline [[lifetree]]s, hoping he would freeze during the night. Even though the lifetree was robbing him of intelligent thought, Calrissian was able to free himself, though his feet were severely [[Frostbite|frostbitten]] and required light [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] treatment in a portable [[gel-bath]] kept on the Falcon. Later, Calrissian and Vuffi Raa investigated the largest pyramid and located the Mindharp, and were promptly teleported back to Rafa IV. [[File:Vuffi.jpg|thumb|right|Vuffi Raa.]] Vuffi Raa, who in fact had been programmed to betray Calrissian before he had even been "won," promptly turned Calrissian in to Governor Mer. Mer took the Mindharp and sentenced Calrissian to a lifetime of hard labor, but Vuffi Raa, having fulfilled his imperative to betray Calrissian, rescued the [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] just as Mer activated the Mindharp. As the two fled Rafa IV, the transdimensional artifact's subharmonic emanations restored the Toka into the Sharu that they once were, and the great pyramids of the Rafa system crumbled, leaving alien cities in their wake. Calrissian and Vuffi Raa entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] with a full hold of the last Rafa [[life-crystal]]s to leave the Rafa system; this valuable cargo netted Calrissian nearly 250,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. ===Further adventures=== Returning to [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], Calrissian bought a used-starship lot from a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], but discovered the business was expensive and difficult to run. He was pondering selling it off when the Empire arrived in the [[Y'Toub system/Legends|system]]. Calrissian donated his inventory to his fellow outlaws, and Vuffi Raa piloted the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] in the resulting [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]].''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' The attack was diverted, but less than ten percent of his stock was still space-worthy. Calrissian sold ninety percent ownership in what was left of his lot to [[Roa]] at a loss.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Calrissian sought a way to recover his losses, and saw an opportunity in the regional sabacc championships that were soon to be held at [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City's]] [[Yarith Bespin/Legends|Yarith Bespin]] [[Casino/Legends|casino]]. In order to raise the ten thousand credit buy-in, Calrissian and Vuffi Raa returned to the Centrality and attempted to haul legitimate cargo; but tariffs, fees, and licensing cost him more than he made, leaving him with nothing. ===Flamewind of Oseon=== [[File:VuffiRaa-EGD.jpg|thumb|left|Vuffi Raa.]] Thus, when an opportunity to play sabacc in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] during the [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]] arose, Calrissian accepted, and Vuffi Raa accompanied him. Soon after arriving at [[Oseon 6845]], Calrissian was assaulted and forced to shoot his assailant in self-defense with his [[Stingbeam/Legends|stingbeam]]. The [[Administrator Senior]] of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon System]], [[Lob Doluff]], informed Calrissian that the penalty for possessing a deadly weapon was [[Death/Legends|death]] by exposure, but since the administrator admired Calrissian, he arranged for him to instead participate in a drug bust against the [[lesai]]-using wealthy industrialist [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]]. Posing as Mutdah's [[Drug dealer|dealer]], Calrissian and Vuffi Raa transported two agents, [[Bassi Vobah]] and [[Waywa Fybot]], through the maddening [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] of the Flamewind to [[Oseon 5792]]. Mutdah turned out to be Rokur Gepta in disguise, who promptly killed Fybot—who was corrupt and had himself killed Vobah—and planned to kill Calrissian after forcing him to endure a machine that made him relive all the painful moments of his life, but the latter was saved by the intervention of Renatasian [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], whose goal was to kill Vuffi Raa. During the chaos surrounding his escape, Calrissian grabbed Mutdah's drug payment—twenty million credits. He deposited small sums in [[Bank/Legends|banks]] on several worlds, including fifty thousand credits into the [[Bank of Aargau/Legends|Bank of Aargau]], which he planned to use for covering the entry fee for the Bespin sabacc tournament. However, his attempt to deposit the bulk of the money on the Centrality's primary financial planet, [[Dela III]], interrupted a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] attack being committed by the "pirate queen" [[Drea Renthal]]. Despite Calrissian's charm, Renthal kept the remainder of Mutdah's money. ===Saving the Oswaft=== [[File:VuffiRaa.jpg|thumb|right|Vuffi Raa standing on only three of his "legs."]] Soon after, Calrissian and Vuffi Raa encountered [[Lehesu]], a young member of the space-dwelling, hyperjump-capable [[Oswaft]] species, whose curiosity had carried it from its home in the [[ThonBoka]] Nebula (or StarCave) to a desolate part of the Centrality. After halting attempts at communication, Lehesu finally communicated to the crew of the ''Millennium Falcon'' his need for sustenance. Calrissian emptied the contents of the ship's recyclers into space, which Lehesu eagerly consumed. Some months later, the Empire and the Centrality [[Battle of ThonBoka|blockaded]] the entrance to the sack-shaped ThonBoka with five hundred [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] modified to contaminate the "[[interstellar plankton]]" drifting into the [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]], intending to starve the entire Oswaft race into extinction. Lehesu used a transceiver provided to him by Vuffi Raa to contact the ''Millennium Falcon'' and plead for help. Calrissian penetrated the blockade, using a ruse that he was a [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] plying his wares to the very ships that were his obstacle. Though the ''Falcon'' was inspected, no one below the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] was informed of the reason for the blockade, so no one suspected that the ship's real cargo was the ordinary waste carried in its trash and toilet recyclers. After working his way to the front of the blockade, Lando made a break for the mouth of the ThonBoka. Vuffi Raa had rigged a device containing metal flakes on the aft hull of the ''Falcon'' to make the ship appear to disintegrate when hit by blasts from the blockade ships. Inside the ThonBoka, they contacted Lehesu and the Oswaft [[Elders (Oswaft)|Elders]], and they began making plans while other Oswaft went to negotiate with the picket fleet. However, as the Empire's goals were complete extermination, the Empire merely killed the negotiators; in the process, one of them "shouted" at the unshielded [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Courteous]]'', destroying it with its [[maser voice-stream]]. The fleet, seeking retribution, raised their [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and entered the ThonBoka, but were called back by Gepta, who sought to destroy Calrissian. When Gepta arrived, the fleet mobilized and entered the nebula. Fortunately, their pause had given Calrissian time to suggest a battle plan. The Oswaft excreted heavy metals from their pores and then hyperjumped, leaving decoys placed between the enemy cruisers and resulting in massive friendly fire casualties among the fleet; meanwhile, they "shouted" at the starfighters, destroying them with their powerful masers. After the fleet's forces had been reduced by eleven percent, the battle ceased, and Gepta offered Calrissian an ultimatum: engage Gepta in personal combat, or he would fire an [[electromagnetic torpedo]] into the nebula, killing all life therein. Calrissian and Vuffi Raa, who was allowed to participate because Gepta believed he could reprogram the droid to betray his master, faced off against the Sorcerer of Tund in the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of space. After much maneuvering and several ploys, Calrissian landed a stray shot in Gepta's ankle as one of Vuffi Raa's detached tentacles engaged Gepta. The Sorcerer's form withered as he cried out, landing a stray shot that injured Vuffi Raa's torso. Calrissian searched the space suit and found an injured Croke where his shot had landed—Rokur Gepta had not been a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], but a Croke from [[Crakull]], a planet in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] which Lando had once visited. Calrissian squeezed the alien until the last Sorcerer of Tund was little more than a stain on his gloves. [[File:VuffiRaaSphere-NEGTD.png|thumb|left|Vuffi Raa in his spherical adult form.]] At that, the [[Battle of ThonBoka]] renewed, but for only a moment; out of nowhere, a multitude of [[Silentium|fifty-kilometer self-aware droids]] appeared, issuing a demand on all channels: "''Cease fire immediately or be destroyed!''". They were [[The One (being)|The One]], [[The Other]] and [[Silentium|The Rest]], who had come for Vuffi Raa. The Imperial fleet, faced with thousands of gigantic ships, retreated from their genocidal mission. Vuffi Raa's progenitors repaired the little droid and departed with him into the Unknown Regions, and Lando departed the ThonBoka with a hold filled with valuable Oswaft-synthesized gemstones. ===Continuing Relationship=== {{Quote|…And Vuffi Raa?''"
"''Yes, Master?''"
"''Don't you think, now that you know exactly who and what you are, that you could stop calling me master?
"Why, I suppose so, Lando. Why didn't you ask me before?|Lando Calrissian and Vuffi Raa after meeting the Silentium.|}} Although Vuffi and his Master parted ways, the droid would again encounter Calrissian on a number of occasions after their last adventure. When [[Kyle Katarn]] met Lando aboard ''[[Star of Empire]]'' in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Calrissian was accompanied by his former droid companion.[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]] ==Personality and traits== Vuffi Raa was programmed by his creators to be completely non-violent, and even piloting a starship in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]] stressed his circuits; this was evidenced by the stress he suffered when piloting the ''Millennium Falcon'' during the defense of Nar Shaddaa. However, he eventually became one of the few droids to overcome his own programming, and became able to inflict injury in the defense of himself and his friends or master. He was very loyal to his owners, particularly Lando, with whom he had developed something of a bond. ==Behind the scenes== Vuffi Raa was created by [[L. Neil Smith]] for the novel ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', and featured prominently in its two sequels, ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' and ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''. [[File:Different_Vuffi_Raa.jpg|thumb|right|A different Vuffi Raa.]] Though the above images are accepted as being the most [[canon]]ical depiction of Vuffi Raa, a different version of him could be seen on the cover of ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', the original source material for the character. Vuffi Raa's "hands," which branch repeatedly from finger-sized appendages down to the microscopic level, were evidently modeled on the concept of the "[[Wikipedia:bush robot|bush robot]]." [[Wikipedia:Hans Moravec|Hans Moravec]] first envisioned the concept of "a robot that looks like a tree, with a big stem, repeatedly branching into thinner, shorter and more numerous twigs, finally ending up in jillions of near-microscopic cilia" in [[1980]], shortly before Smith began writing his books.{{WebCite|url=http://www.frc.ri.cmu.edu/~hpm/project.archive/robot.papers/1980/bush|text=Bush Robots] The concept of the bush robot has continued to be explored, and in [[1999]] Moravec and Jesse Easudes presented a final report to [[Wikipedia:NASA|NASA]|work="Fractal branching ultra-dexterous robots."|archivedate=20010822022530}} {{WebCite|url=http://www.frc.ri.cmu.edu/users/hpm/project.archive/robot.papers/1999/NASA.report.99/9901.NASA.html|text=Fractal branching ultra-dexterous robots (Bush robots)|archivedate=20020122042925}} Though the character of Vuffi as he originally was wasn't incorporated into the post-[[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] [[canon]], the character of [[L3-37]] was made as a replacement for Vuffi in the canon journal book ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story: Tales from Vandor]]'', where L3-37 briefly took on the name of Vuffi and had many of the same adventures with Lando that Vuffi had. ==Appearances== *''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' *''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]] *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080522/index.html|text="Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array"|int=Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array|newhost=Suvudu}} {{Mo}} *[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] {{Ab|Millennium Falcon (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Vuffi Raa}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *{{CollectorCite|5|Use the Force|Use the Force: A Novel Trivia Quiz}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *{{FactFile|126|VUF 1-2|Vuffi Raa}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Starships & Vehicles Collection 1|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 1]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Neil Smith}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Vuffi Raa |nl=Vuffi Raa }} [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:Manumitted droids]] [[Category:Silentium]]
